flashcards.front: What is a multi-tape Turing machine?
flashcards.back: A multi-tape Turing machine is an extension of the basic Turing machine that uses multiple tapes instead of a single tape.

flashcards.front: What is a multi-head Turing machine?
flashcards.back: A multi-head Turing machine is an extension of the basic Turing machine that uses multiple heads instead of a single head.

flashcards.front: What is a non-deterministic Turing machine?
flashcards.back: A non-deterministic Turing machine is an extension of the basic Turing machine that allows the machine to make choices during its computation.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of a multi-tape Turing machine?
flashcards.back: The main advantage of a multi-tape Turing machine is that it is more powerful and flexible than a single-tape Turing machine.

flashcards.front: What is the main disadvantage of a non-deterministic Turing machine?
flashcards.back: The main disadvantage of a non-deterministic Turing machine is that it is less predictable and harder to analyze than a deterministic Turing machine.
